time) shall continue to be eligible for such categorisation under these Directions, till
maturity.

controls.control_objective: To ensure include dairy, fisheries, animal husbandry, poultry, bee-keeping, sericulture and similar activities
controls.control_description:
activities allied to agriculture shall include dairy, fisheries,
animal husbandry, poultry, bee-keeping, sericulture and similar activities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include dairy, fisheries, animal husbandry, poultry, bee-keeping, sericulture and similar activities

controls.control_objective: To ensure include individual farmers including small and marginal farmers1 (smfs), proprietorship firms of farmers directly engaged in agriculture and allied activities, and self-help groups (shgs) or joint liability groups (jlgs) i.e., group of individual farmers
controls.control_description:
(ii) Non-Corporate Farmers (NCF) shall include individual farmers including Small
and Marginal Farmers1 (SMFs), proprietorship firms of farmers directly engaged
in agriculture and allied activities, and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) or Joint Liability
Groups (JLGs) i.e., group of individual farmers, provided banks maintain
disaggregated data of such loans.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include individual farmers including small and marginal farmers1 (smfs), proprietorship firms of farmers directly engaged in agriculture and allied activities, and self-help groups (shgs) or joint liability groups (jlgs) i.e., group of individual farmers

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to be eligible for such categorisation under these directions till maturity
controls.control_description:
4.3 All loans categorised as Priority Sector Lending (PSL) under the erstwhile
Master Directions on PSL dated September 04, 2020 (updated as on June 21,
2024) shall continue to be eligible for such categorisation under these
Directions till maturity.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to be eligible for such categorisation under these directions till maturity

controls.control_objective: To ensure be computed as follows: bank credit in india [as prescribed in item no
controls.control_description:
6.1 For the purpose of priority sector lending, ANBC shall be computed as follows:
Bank Credit in India [as prescribed in item No.VI of Form `A' under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934] I
Bills rediscounted with RBI and other approved Financial Institutions II Net Bank Credit (NBC)* III(I-II) Outstanding Deposits under RIDF and other eligible funds with NABARD, NHB, SIDBI and MUDRA Ltd in lieu of non-achievement of priority sector lending targets/sub-targets + outstanding PSLCs
IV
Eligible amount for exemptions on issuance of long-term bonds for infrastructure and affordable housing as per [Reserve Bank of India (Resource Raising Norms) Directions, 2025 as applicable to Commercial Banks and Small Finance Banks]2
V
Advances extended in India against the incremental FCNR (B)/NRE deposits, qualifying for exemption from CRR/SLR requirements, as per the Reserve Bank's circulars DBOD.No.Ret.BC.36/12.01.001/2013-14 dated August 14, 2013 read with DBOD.No.Ret.BC.93/12.01.001/2013- 14 dated January 31, 2014, DBOD mailbox clarification issued on February 6, 2014 and UBD.BPD.(PCB).CIR.No.5/13.01.000/2013-14 dated August 27, 2013 read with UBD.BPD.(PCB).Cir.No.72/13.01.000/ 2013-14 dated June 11, 2014.3
VI
Investments made by public sector banks in the Recapitalization Bonds floated by Government of India
VII
Other investments eligible to be treated as priority sector (e.g.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be computed as follows: bank credit in india [as prescribed in item no

controls.control_objective: To ensure be ensured that the credit to priority sector and all sub-sectors so written off shall also be subtracted category wise from priority sector target an
controls.control_description:

off at Corporate/Head Office level, it shall be ensured that the credit to priority
sector and all sub-sectors so written off shall also be subtracted category wise
from priority sector target and sub-target achievement.

controls.control_objective: To ensure be added to the anbc in the denominator for computation of psl targets
controls.control_description: However, foreign banks with less than 20 branches have an option to reckon the CEOBSE extended to borrowers for eligible priority sector activities for achievement of priority sector target, subject to the condition that the CEOBSE (both priority sector and non-priority sector excluding interbank exposure) shall be added to the ANBC in the denominator for computation of PSL targets.

controls.control_objective: To ensure be assigned to the incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the credit flow is comparatively lower (per capita psl less th
controls.control_description:
With effect from FY 2024-25, a higher weight (125%) shall be
assigned to the incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the
credit flow is comparatively lower (per capita PSL less than ₹9,000), and a lower
weight (90%) will be assigned for incremental priority sector credit in the identified
districts where the credit flow is comparatively higher (per capita PSL greater than
₹42,000).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be assigned to the incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the credit flow is comparatively lower (per capita psl less th

controls.control_objective: To ensure prescribe the time limit within which the bank communicates its decision in writing to the applicants
controls.control_description:
Bank Boards
shall prescribe the time limit within which the bank communicates its decision in
writing to the applicants.
controls.implementation_method: Implement technical controls to configure system to prescribe the time limit within which the bank communicates its decision in writing to the applicants

controls.control_objective: To ensure ensure that loans categorised as priority sector lending are granted for approved purposes and the end use is monitored, by putting in place proper in
controls.control_description:
(v) Banks shall ensure that loans categorised as priority sector lending are granted for
approved purposes and the end use is monitored, by putting in place proper
internal systems and controls.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to ensure that loans categorised as priority sector lending are granted for approved purposes and the end use is monitored, by putting in place proper in

controls.control_objective: To ensure be classified only in any one of the eight identified categories specified in para 5 of these master directions
controls.control_description:
(vi) Each priority sector loan shall be classified only in any one of the eight identified
categories specified in para 5 of these Master Directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be classified only in any one of the eight identified categories specified in para 5 of these master directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure be issued by the bank
controls.control_description:
iv) Sellers/Buyers: Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs), Regional Rural Banks
(RRBs), Local Area Banks (LABs), Small Finance Banks (SFBs) and Urban Co-
operative Banks (UCBs) who have originated PSL eligible category loans subject
to such regulations as may be issued by the Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be issued by the bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure to achieve specified sub-target for lending to small and marginal farmers
controls.control_description:
In addition to the overall
<image>
target and sectoral targets for lending to agriculture and micro enterprises, banks
are required to achieve specified sub-target for lending to Small and Marginal
Farmers.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to achieve specified sub-target for lending to small and marginal farmers

controls.control_objective: To ensure buy any of the available pslcs
controls.control_description:
However, if a bank
is having shortfall in achievement of the overall target only, as applicable to it, may
buy any of the available PSLCs.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to buy any of the available pslcs

controls.control_objective: To ensure have met the priority sector target by way of the sum of outstanding priority sector lending portfolio and net of pslcs issued and purchased
controls.control_description:
However, as on the
reporting date, the bank must have met the priority sector target by way of the sum
of outstanding priority sector lending portfolio and net of PSLCs issued and
purchased.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have met the priority sector target by way of the sum of outstanding priority sector lending portfolio and net of pslcs issued and purchased

controls.control_objective: To ensure be required to invest in ridf/other funds as hitherto
controls.control_description:
To the extent of shortfall in the achievement of target, banks may be
required to invest in RIDF/other funds as hitherto.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be required to invest in ridf/other funds as hitherto

controls.control_objective: To ensure report the amount of pslcs (category- wise) sold and purchased during the year in the 'disclosures to the balance sheet'
controls.control_description:
xiv) Disclosures: Both seller and buyer shall report the amount of PSLCs (category-
wise) sold and purchased during the year in the 'Disclosures to the Balance
Sheet'.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to report the amount of pslcs (category- wise) sold and purchased during the year in the 'disclosures to the balance sheet'

controls.control_objective: To ensure sell pslcs with a nominal value of ₹100 crores to bank b on july 15
controls.control_description: Bank A may sell PSLCs with a nominal value of ₹100 crores to Bank B on July 15,
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to sell pslcs with a nominal value of ₹100 crores to bank b on july 15

controls.control_objective: To ensure buy ₹100 crore pslc on march 30, 2026 from bank d
controls.control_description: Bank C may buy ₹100 crore PSLC on March 30, 2026 from Bank D.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to buy ₹100 crore pslc on march 30, 2026 from bank d

controls.control_objective: To ensure be available to outstanding credit extended under the measures specified below:
controls.control_description:
Priority sector
classification shall be available to outstanding credit extended under the measures
specified below:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be available to outstanding credit extended under the measures specified below:

controls.control_objective: To ensure 7, 2021, an on-tap liquidity window of ₹50,000 crore with tenors of up to three years at the repo rate till march 31, 2022 was opened to boost provisi
controls.control_description:
(i) In terms of press release: 2021-2022/177 dated May 7, 2021, an on-tap
liquidity window of ₹50,000 crore with tenors of up to three years at the repo
rate till March 31, 2022 was opened to boost provision of immediate liquidity
for ramping up COVID-related healthcare infrastructure and services in the
country.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to 7, 2021, an on-tap liquidity window of ₹50,000 crore with tenors of up to three years at the repo rate till march 31, 2022 was opened to boost provisi
